interleaving_reachable: formal cover PASS: c0_v && c1_v && (c0_key != c1_key) reached at step 3 (two contexts live with different keys)

contracts.claim: a context NOT written this cycle is byte-for-byte unchanged (cross-context isolation)
a new-key allocation lands on a FREE slot, never overwrites a live different-key context
new-key start with no free slot changes nothing and drops
SOM for an already-active key aborts that context (drop), never silently appends
an out-of-sequence continuation aborts its own context and never commits

gap_found_via_mutation: ALLOC mutant initially SURVIVED: it forced all allocations to slot0 so slot1 never filled, so 'table full' never occurred and C-ALLOC-FULL never triggered; and no contract said 'allocation must target a free slot'. Added C-ALLOC-NEW (written slot must have been free) -> mutant now killed by both lanes.
caveats: CONTEXT_COUNT=2 (enough to prove interleaving isolation; more contexts = same shape, larger state).
Payload is a byte count; SRAM byte-exact packing + descriptor + header-snapshot queue are separate v3 stages not yet reflected.
KEY-UNIQUE (no two live contexts share a key) is maintained by construction but left as a cover, not a proven inductive invariant (would need an inductive strengthening).
